2606.19815.title: Clusters are All You Need: Pre-Training the Tsetlin Machine with Semantic Clusters from Language Models for Interpretability
2606.19815.paper_summary: Pre-trained language models such as BERT achieve strong text classification performance but lack transparency, limiting their use in high-stakes settings. The Tsetlin Machine (TM) offers fully interpretable, clause-based reasoning but captures little semantic information, and prior attempts to bridge the two rely on static word embeddings that miss contextual meaning. We propose a semantic pre-training framework that transfers knowledge from a pre-trained language model into a TM without using embeddings. Text samples are grouped into semantically coherent clusters with K-means or Top2Vec, and the resulting cluster-sample pairs pre-train a non-negated TM with enhanced Type I feedback. The TM thereby learns interpretable semantic keywords that are fine-tuned on downstream tasks. Across five datasets, our method substantially outperforms vanilla and embedding-based TMs and reaches performance competitive with BERT while remaining interpretable.
2606.19815.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練語言模型如 BERT 在文本分類性能上表現優異，但缺乏透明度，限制了它們在高風險環境中的使用。Tsetlin 機器 (TM) 提供完全可解釋的基於條款的推理，但捕捉的語義信息很少，之前試圖將兩者結合的嘗試依賴於靜態詞嵌入，這忽略了上下文意義。我們提出了一個語義預訓練框架，將預訓練語言模型的知識轉移到 TM 中，而不使用嵌入。文本樣本被分組為語義一致的集群，使用 K-means 或 Top2Vec，生成的集群-樣本對預訓練一個非否定的 TM，並增強了 I 類反饋。因此，TM 學會了可解釋的語義關鍵詞，並在下游任務上進行微調。在五個數據集上，我們的方法顯著超越了普通和基於嵌入的 TM，並達到了與 BERT 競爭的性能，同時保持可解釋性。
2606.19815.author: Jiechao Gao et.al.
2606.19815.authors: Jiechao Gao, Rohan Kumar Yadav, Yuangang Li, Yuandong Pan, Jie Wang, Ying Liu, Michael Lepech
